channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: DOJ Returns Villanueva Flood Control Cases to Ombudsman for Review
item.description: The Department of Justice (DOJ) has formally endorsed the flood control-related cases involving former Senator Villanueva back to the Office of the Ombudsman. These cases, which have been under scruti...

item.title: Reform UK Leads in YouGov Poll Amid Methodology Debate
item.description: The latest YouGov/Sky News/Times voting intention poll shows Reform UK leading with 25%, followed by the Green Party at 19%, and both the Conservatives and Labour tied at 17%. The Liberal Democrats ar...

item.title: Nvidia Introduces NemoClaw to Improve Security in Its OpenClaw AI Platform
item.description: During Nvidia’s 2026 GTC keynote, the company unveiled NemoClaw, a new security-focused stack designed to make its autonomous AI agent platform, OpenClaw, safer and more reliable. According to Nvidia,...

item.title: Many Tanzanian secondary schools face shortage of science laboratories
item.description: About two out of every five government secondary schools in Tanzania do not have proper science labs, making it hard for students to do practical experiments in biology, chemistry, and physics. Accord...

item.title: Pep Guardiola insists Manchester City players believe they can overturn 3-0 deficit against Real Madrid
item.description: Manchester City manager Pep Guardiola has expressed strong confidence that his team can mount a comeback in their UEFA Champions League Round of 16 second leg against Real Madrid at the Etihad Stadium...

item.title: Experts warn of security risks in proposed online English tests for UK migrants
item.description: The Home Office plans to move English language tests for migrants seeking visas to a fully digital format under a new £816 million contract, potentially operational by December 2026. This would allow ...

item.title: Life in Tehran Amid Conflict: Government Stability and Civilian Struggles
item.description: Sky News reports from Tehran, where the city shows visible signs of war yet the Iranian government maintains strong control. The capital remains tense and subdued, with citizens coping with missile st...

item.title: Philippine embassy provides legal help to 2 Filipinas arrested in UAE over missile attack videos
item.description: The Philippine Embassy in the United Arab Emirates (UAE) is extending legal assistance to two Filipinas who were arrested in Dubai for allegedly recording and sharing videos of missile attacks carried...

item.title: Pregnant YouTuber Allegedly Killed by Husband in Jagtial District
item.description: In a tragic incident reported from Madhapur village of Korutla mandal in Jagtial district, Telangana, a young woman named Vaishnavi, aged 19 and four months pregnant, was allegedly stabbed to death by...

item.title: Tinubu Swears In Oyedele As Minister of State for Finance, Charges Him on Economic Growth
item.description: Yesterday for Abuja, President Bola Ahmed Tinubu don officially swear in Dr Taiwo Oyedele as the new Minister of State for Finance. Tinubu yan say him pick Oyedele because of him strong background for...

item.title: Government Introduces Easier Compensation Claims and Railcard Verification to Prevent Fare Fraud
item.description: The UK government is introducing new measures to make train travel more transparent and fair for passengers. Starting this April, discounted tickets purchased with railcards will undergo additional ve...

item.title: Sowore Says Peter Obi and Bala Mohammed Are Not Real Opposition Figures
item.description: Omoyele Sowore, former presidential candidate of the African Action Congress (AAC), don talk say former Anambra Governor, Peter Obi, and Bauchi State Governor, Bala Mohammed, no be opposition politici...

item.title: Temporary Restraining Order Issued Against Huda Mustafa Following Alleged Threats to Boyfriend’s Family
item.description: Huda Mustafa, known for her appearance on 'Love Island,' has been issued a temporary restraining order due to allegations made by Nicole Olivera, the former partner of Mustafa’s boyfriend, Louis Russe...

item.title: Learning Swahili: Meaning and Use of the Word 'Msikiti'
item.description: This article explains the meaning and origin of the Swahili word 'msikiti,' which refers to a mosque, a place where Muslims perform prayers and worship God. The word is derived from Arabic 'masjid,' m...

item.title: Researchers develop computational method to design efficient sunlight-driven catalysts
item.description: Scientists at the Helmholtz-Zentrum Dresden-Rossendorf have created an innovative computational framework that could greatly accelerate the discovery of new materials able to transform sunlight into u...

item.title: Lacoste Unveils a Rain-Inspired Palette at Paris Fashion Week 2026
item.description: Lacoste presented its Fall/Winter 2026 collection at Paris Fashion Week, blending its signature sporty roots with refined, weather-ready elegance. The collection, designed by creative director Pelagia...

item.title: New Taskforce Launched to Improve NHS Maternity and Neonatal Services in England
item.description: The UK government has established a new taskforce aimed at enhancing the quality of maternity and neonatal care within the NHS in England. Chaired by Health Secretary Wes Streeting, the 17-member grou...

item.title: Canadian Football Hall of Famer Tom Brown Dies at Age 89
item.description: Tom Brown, a celebrated Canadian Football Hall of Famer and three-time CFL all-star, has passed away at the age of 89, according to the B.C. Lions. Brown, a key defensive lineman, played 97 regular-se...

item.title: RBA raises cash rate to 4.1% after divided board decision
item.description: The Reserve Bank of Australia (RBA) has lifted the cash rate by 25 basis points to 4.1%, following a closely split vote among the nine-member Monetary Policy Board. Five members supported the increase...

item.title: Pakistan’s Rooftop Solar Expansion Reduces Dependence on Imported Energy
item.description: Pakistan’s rapid adoption of rooftop solar panels has provided a significant buffer against energy shocks caused by disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz. A recent study by Renewables First and the Cent...
